scheme_name: Chief Minister EBC Civil Seva Protsahan Yojana
page_title: Chief Minister EBC Civil Seva Protsahan Yojana

details: Chief Minister EBC Civil Seva Protsahan Yojana

" has been initiated by the Backward Classes and Extremely Backward Classes Welfare Department, Government of Bihar. Under this scheme, the Bihar Government provides incentives to individuals belonging to the Extremely Backward Classes upon passing the preliminary examination of various competitive exams such as UPSC, BPSC, Bihar Judicial Service, NDA, CDS, Banking, Railway, SSC, etc., to support their further preparation for the main examination/interview. The scheme is designed to provide financial assistance to students from the Most Backward Classes (MBC) who are preparing for civil services exams.
benefits: Under this scheme, candidates who pass the preliminary examination of competitive exams are provided with a prescribed incentive amount ranging from ₹30,000/- to ₹1,00,000/- as under:
Name of the Institution Conducting the Competitive Examination
Union Public Service Commission, New Delhi
Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI Dy. S.P.)
National Defence Academy (NDA) and Naval Academy first stage (written exam) exam
Combined Competitive (Preliminary) Examination conducted by Bihar Public Service Commission, Patna
State Civil Services Combined Competitive (Preliminary) Examination conducted by other States
State Public Service Commissions of the respective states
Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Grade-B Officer Preliminary Exam
Preliminary Examination for Bank Probationary Officer in State Bank of India (SBI) and other Scheduled Commercial Banks
State Bank of India/Institute of Banking Personnel Selection (IBPS)
Preliminary Examination for Assistant Administrative Officer in Life Insurance Corporation of India
Central Staff Selection Commission, New Delhi
Amount payable on passing the Technical and Combined Graduate Level Preliminary examination conducted by various Railway Recruitment Boards
eligibility: The applicant should be a permanent resident of Bihar.
The applicant should belong to the Extremely Backward Class (EBC) category.
The applicant should have passed the preliminary examination of a competitive exam, such as UPSC, BPSC, Bihar Judicial Service, NDA, CDS, Banking, Railway, SSC, etc.
The applicant should not have previously received the same incentive from any other source.

documents_required: At the time of filling of application form, the applicant's Photo size should be less than 50 kb. (Preferred Dimension : 200 x 230 px)
At the time of filling of application form, the applicant's Signature size should be less than 20 kb. (Preferred Dimension : 140 x 60 px)

faqs.question: Who is eligible for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Applicants must be permanent residents of Bihar, belong to the EBC category, and have passed the preliminary exam of a recognized competitive exam (e.g., UPSC, BPSC, SSC). Additionally, they should not have previously received this incentive from any other source.

faqs.question: What are the benefits of this scheme?
faqs.answer: Successful candidates can receive an incentive ranging from ₹30,000 to ₹1,00,000, depending on the competitive exam they have passed.

faqs.question: Which competitive exams are covered under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Exams such as UPSC, BPSC, Bihar Judicial Service, NDA, CDS, Banking, Railway, SSC, and other recognized exams are covered under this scheme.

faqs.question: How do I apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: You can apply by visiting the official website: https://bcebconline.bih.nic.in/.

faqs.question: Is there an age limit to apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: No specific age limit is mentioned, but you must meet the eligibility criteria of the competitive exam you have passed.

faqs.question: Are candidates who are employed in government jobs eligible?
faqs.answer: No, candidates already employed in any government department, public sector undertaking, or state-funded institution are not eligible for the scheme.

faqs.question: Can I apply for the scheme if I have already received a similar incentive from another source?
faqs.answer: No, you cannot apply if you have previously received the same incentive from any other source.

faqs.question: How much incentive is provided for passing the UPSC preliminary exam?
faqs.answer: The incentive amount for passing the UPSC preliminary exam can go up to ₹1,00,000.

faqs.question: Do I need to submit proof of passing the preliminary exam?
faqs.answer: Yes, you will need to submit proof of having passed the preliminary exam of the competitive exam.

faqs.question: Can I apply multiple times if I pass different exams?
faqs.answer: No, the incentive is provided only once. You cannot apply again for a different exam after receiving the incentive.
